candidate_problems.text: How can 'counterfactual structure' be defined and measured non-circularly, so that understanding is distinguishable from mere predictive compression without presupposing an intervention framework already built from explanatory assumptions?

What precise criteria separate genuinely generative compression from spurious or overfit compression, especially in domains where many compact models fit observations and some interventions but differ on unobserved variations?

How does the requirement that explanation be communicable and criticizable relate to private or tacit model competence: can an agent count as understanding if it reliably supports interventions but cannot render its model into an explicit form usable for criticism and transfer?
